Title: Biosynthesis of the anti-diabetic metabolite montbretin A: glucosylation of the central intermediate mini-MbA

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-dependent glycosyltransferase, UGT703E1, clade D glycoside glucosyltransferase (GGT)
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late glycosylation steps of acylated flavonol glycoside assembly
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes 1,2-glucosylation of the central intermediate mini-MbA using UDP-Glc to form myricetin 3-O-(glucosyl-6′-O-caffeoyl)-glucosyl rhamnoside (MbA-XR2)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potent and selective inhibitor of human pancreatic α-amylase; anti-diabetic/anti-obesity metabolite
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes MbA biosynthesis by converting mini-MbA to the fourth pathway intermediate MbA-XR2; enables production of MbA-XR2 and MbB-XR2 in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CcUGT3 was identified by co-expression with known MbA pathway genes and strong enrichment in young corms relative to old corms. 2. Recombinant CcUGT3/UGT703E1 specifically converted mini-MbA plus UDP-Glc into MbA-XR2, whereas the paralog UGT703E2 showed no activity. 3. CcUGT3 transcripts peak in young corms during early summer, matching MbA accumulation. 4. Transient co-expression of CcUGT3 with upstream pathway genes in Nicotiana benthamiana validated in planta formation of MbA-XR2 and MbB-XR2.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Corm protein extract assays; transcriptome co-expression analysis; recombinant protein enzyme assay with LC-MS/MS; substrate specificity assay; qRT-PCR; transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in young corms, especially June; over 28-fold higher in young versus old corms at peak time; low in old corms
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In Nicotiana benthamiana leaves, function validated by transient co-expression under 35S promoter
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developmental stage of corms (young versus old; early summer peak); transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter (constitutive) in Nicotiana benthamiana transient expression
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomics/transcriptome co-expression and differential expression; NCBI SRA SRP108844
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful enzyme for metabolic engineering of MbA production; should be stacked with upstream myricetin and mini-MbA pathway genes and with future downstream UGTs to complete MbA biosynthesis; engineering efforts must also improve caffeoyl-CoA supply to favor MbA over MbB
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: In heterologous Nicotiana benthamiana, pathway flux can be diverted toward MbB-related products due to limited caffeoyl-CoA and competing coumaroyl-CoA-derived acylation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CcUGT3 does not accept MRG lacking the caffeoyl group, indicating acyl-group-dependent acceptor recognition. It can also use UDP-Xyl and UDP-Rha with mini-MbA in vitro, but prefers UDP-Glc. The paper establishes this as step 4 of a six-step linear MbA pathway.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-dependent glycosyltransferase, UGT703E2, clade D
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late glycosylation candidate for mini-MbA conversion
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Tested as candidate glucosyltransferase for mini-MbA glucosylation but no detectable activity was observed with mini-MbA and UDP-Glc
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potent and selective inhibitor of human pancreatic α-amylase; anti-diabetic/anti-obesity metabolite
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: No detectable contribution to mini-MbA glucosylation under tested conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT703E2 was cloned as a close paralog of UGT703E1 from montbretia corm cDNA. 2. Recombinant UGT703E2 showed no activity with mini-MbA and UDP-Glc. 3. Therefore UGT703E2 was excluded as the step-4 mini-MbA glucosyltransferase.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Candidate cloning and recombinant protein enzyme assay with LC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Limited direct value for MbA engineering based on lack of detected step-4 activity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although highly similar to UGT703E1, UGT703E2 was not functionally validated for MbA biosynthesis in this study.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports heterologous production of myricetin-derived montbretin intermediates when co-expressed with pathway genes in Nicotiana benthamiana
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter (constitutive) in Nicotiana benthamiana transient expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana as part of reconstructed upstream pathway
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful auxiliary regulator for pathway reconstruction because it supports myricetin precursor formation in heterologous systems
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaf (heterologous transient expression system); young corms in source species
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CcMYB4 was one of three montbretia myricetin biosynthesis genes used to reconstruct upstream steps in Nicotiana benthamiana. 2. Its co-expression enabled production of mini-MbA/mini-MbB precursors required for validating CcUGT3 in planta. 3. The present paper did not newly characterize its direct regulatory targets.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves under 35S promoter
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Upstream enabling regulator for precursor supply in heterologous pathway reconstruction, but not the focus of mechanistic analysis in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: precursor biosynthesis for flavonol-derived montbretin pathway
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potent and selective inhibitor of human pancreatic α-amylase; anti-diabetic/anti-obesity metabolite
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included here only because it was part of the experimental reconstitution system; direct regulatory causality was established in previous work, not in this paper.
